title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn, Jules, and Jeeves interview Edith Pargeter to gather information regarding Gwendolyn's childhood and the disappearance of her stepmother, Helen Kennedy (also known as Mrs. Hardy or Lady Hildred). Edith recalls Gwendolyn’s former nanny, Léonie, and a maid named Lily Abbot, who used to play games with Gwendolyn. Edith describes Helen as a kind person who was essentially a child herself, noting that Helen's brother, Ken Kennedy, was much older and reclusive.

The conversation shifts to the circumstances surrounding Helen's sudden disappearance, an event that caused Colonel Hardy to collapse. Jules and Jeeves explain they are investigating her status for legal reasons. Edith reveals that Lily Abbot had long suspected an affair between Helen and an unnamed man whose name began with "E" or "H." This man and his wife, who were summer visitors from Northumberland staying at the Royal Clarence Hotel sixteen years ago, allegedly had an interest in Helen.

Edith also discloses that Helen had planned to move to a house in Norfolk and had even invited Edith to join her. This move was kept secret, as Helen seemed to have developed a sudden aversion to Dilham and appeared afraid to stay there. The interview concludes with Edith recalling a moment when Lily Abbot reported hearing a mysterious lady and gentleman talking in the sitting room after returning from the garden.
